key_aspects: Production-hardened security settings with DEBUG=False
Required environment variables validation (DJANGO_SECRET_KEY, DATABASE_URL)
PostgreSQL database configuration with connection pooling (conn_max_age=600)
Redis caching with fallback to LocMemCache
HTTPS enforcement with HSTS (31536000 seconds = 1 year)
Security headers: XSS protection, CSP-ready, referrer policy
Secure cookie settings (SECURE, HTTPONLY, SAMESITE)
JSON-formatted logging for aggregation and monitoring
Sentry integration for error tracking with 10% transaction sampling
Celery configuration with task time limits (5min soft, 10min hard)
REST Framework hardening: JSONRenderer only, throttling (50/hour anon, 1000/hour user)
Admin email handlers for ERROR-level security and request issues
Staging-specific configuration that mirrors production closely
DEBUG=False for production-like behavior
Staging domain configuration (staging.e-menum.com)
Database configuration similar to production
Caching and Redis setup for staging environment
Security settings balancing testing needs with safety
Reduced logging verbosity compared to development
Optional Sentry integration with warning if not configured

patterns_discovered.description: Both files import base.py settings and override specific values for their environment. This follows Django best practices for multi-environment setups.
Production settings validate critical environment variables at import time with descriptive error messages, preventing silent failures.
Non-critical settings (Redis, Sentry, Email) log warnings when not configured rather than failing, allowing partial functionality.
patterns_discovered.code_example: logger.warning('REDIS_URL not set in production. Using local memory cache...')

patterns_discovered.description: All security-related settings default to most restrictive values: SECURE_SSL_REDIRECT=True, X_FRAME_OPTIONS='DENY', CSRF_COOKIE_SECURE=True, etc.
Production uses JSON-formatted logging for better log aggregation and monitoring in production environments.
Production database configuration includes connection pooling with health checks and SSL requirements.
Production includes multi-integration Sentry setup with Django, Celery, and Redis integrations plus selective sampling rates.

gotchas_discovered.description: The staging.py file appears truncated in the diff output (ends mid-line in CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS configuration). This suggests the file creation may be incomplete or the diff was cut off.
gotchas_discovered.impact: Staging environment may have incomplete configuration if file is truly truncated

gotchas_discovered.description: ALLOWED_HOSTS and CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS have hardcoded default domains ('e-menum.com', 'www.e-menum.com'). These should only be defaults for development or should be completely env-var dependent.
gotchas_discovered.impact: If env vars aren't set, requests to wrong domains could be accepted in production

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Fallback to LocMemCache without deprecation path
gotchas_discovered.description: Production falls back to LocMemCache if Redis is unavailable, which is problematic for multi-process deployments and defeats the purpose of requiring Redis.
gotchas_discovered.impact: Cache inconsistency in production multi-process deployments

gotchas_discovered.description: CELERY_BROKER_URL and CELERY_RESULT_BACKEND are optional env vars with no validation or fallback explanation, unlike DATABASE_URL which raises explicit errors.
gotchas_discovered.impact: Celery tasks may silently fail if broker URLs are not configured

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Sentry integration with 10% sampling by default
gotchas_discovered.description: traces_sample_rate=0.1 means 90% of transactions won't be tracked for performance monitoring, which may hide performance issues.
gotchas_discovered.impact: Production performance issues may not be adequately tracked

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: PII protection disabled in logging but Sentry sends default_pii=False
gotchas_discovered.description: While Sentry has send_default_pii=False, logging handlers and some integrations may still capture sensitive data in JSON format without explicit filtering.
gotchas_discovered.impact: Potential for accidental PII in logs despite Sentry protection

approach_outcome.summary: Successfully created production and staging environment-specific settings files
accomplishments: Created comprehensive production.py with 392 lines of hardened security and performance configurations
Created staging.py with production-mirroring configuration (233 lines, though appears incomplete/truncated)
Implemented environment variable validation for critical settings (SECRET_KEY, DATABASE_URL)
Added production-optimized logging with JSON formatting
Integrated Sentry error tracking with appropriate sampling rates
Configured security headers: HSTS (1 year), XSS protection, CSP-ready, secure cookies
Set up database connection pooling and health checks
Implemented graceful degradation for optional services (Redis, Sentry, Email)
Applied DRY principle by inheriting from base.py settings
Structured Celery, CORS, and REST Framework configurations for production
missing_elements: Staging.py file appears truncated - configuration may be incomplete
No validation or requirements for Celery broker URLs (DATABASE_URL has validation)
Development settings file not created (only production and staging)
Static file serving strategy incomplete (WhiteNoise commented out)
Media storage strategy incomplete (S3 implementation commented out)

recommendations.rationale: The staging.py file appears truncated in the diff. Ensure all necessary configuration is present for a proper staging environment.

recommendations.description: Add environment variable validation for CELERY_BROKER_URL similar to DATABASE_URL
recommendations.rationale: Production critical services should fail explicitly if not configured, not silently. Celery broker URL should be required in production like DATABASE_URL.

recommendations.description: Remove hardcoded default domains from ALLOWED_HOSTS and CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS
recommendations.rationale: Production should require explicit env var configuration for allowed hosts rather than having built-in defaults that could mask configuration issues.

recommendations.description: Remove LocMemCache fallback for Redis or make it fail explicitly
recommendations.rationale: Multi-process production deployments need distributed caching. Silent fallback to LocMemCache causes cache inconsistency bugs that are hard to diagnose.

recommendations.description: Increase Sentry traces_sample_rate from 0.1 to 0.5 or implement dynamic sampling
recommendations.rationale: 10% sampling rate means 90% of performance data is lost. For production monitoring, 50% or dynamic sampling based on transaction duration would be more appropriate.

recommendations.description: Create development.py settings file with DEBUG=True and relaxed security
recommendations.rationale: Complete the three-environment pattern with development settings for local development with debugging capabilities.

recommendations.description: Add a DEPLOYMENT.md file documenting all required environment variables
recommendations.rationale: Complex settings with many required env vars need clear documentation of: variable names, required status, default values, and validation rules.

recommendations.description: Uncomment and activate WhiteNoise static file storage configuration
recommendations.rationale: Current configuration has WhiteNoise commented out. If using it, enable it or document why it's disabled.
